news.title: Unseasonal rains damage rabi crops on 2.49 lakh ha, wheat most affected: Agri Minister
news.description: IMD forecasts more rain from April 9-15 due to Western Disturbances over Jammu & Kashmir, cyclonic circulations over Uttar Pradesh, Bangladesh, Assam and Odisha, and associated troughs

news.title: West Asia conflict deals a double whammy to India’s farm sector: pre-harvest cost pressures, post-harvest shipping delays
news.description: Supply shocks, export disruptions, and labour shortages caused by the Iran war are beginning to strain India’s agriculture despite ample foodgrain stocks.

news.title: Centre kicks off procurement season with 18,000 tonnes of wheat, targets 303 lakh tonnes
news.description: The Centre initiates wheat procurement season, aiming for 303 lakh tonnes amid low prices and adverse weather concerns.

news.title: Wheat procurement in UP starts today, govt says 3,574 purchase centres already operational
news.description: The drive to continue till June 15; 2.24 lakh farmers already got registered to sell produce, say officials

news.title: India’s wheat output may be hit in Punjab, Haryana & UP due to unseasonal rain
news.description: Unseasonal rains in India may cause 6-8% wheat crop loss in Punjab, Haryana, and UP, impacting quality and market prices.

news.title: UP hikes wheat MSP by Rs 160; procurement from March 30
news.description: Cabinet approves setting up of 20 MW floating solar power plant in Gorakhpur

news.title: From Buddha rice to zero-pesticide chana: How farmer-run companies are finding their edge
news.description: Tired of being at the mercy of traders, some small farmers are flipping the script. Through farmer producer organizations and niche produce, they’re evolving from price-takers into savvy board members. Discover the grit behind India’s agrarian business revolu…

agmarknet.title: District-wise Wholesale Prices Monthly Analysis for Wheat in Uttar Pradesh - April, 2026
